name: Hyoscine
indications:
## Main indications

Spasmolytic, to inhibit secretion production.

> Adjust according to institutional protocol and medical prescription.

pediatrico.dose: Antisecretory (adjunct): IV-SC-IM: Infants and < 6 years: 0.3-0.6 mg/kg/dose, maximum dose: 1.5 mg/kg/day; 6 to 12 years: 5 - 10 mg/dose up to 3 times per day, > 12 years: 20 mg/dose up to 4 times per day, adults maximum dose: 100 mg/day. PO: 6 to 12 years: 10 mg/dose every 8 h, > 12 years: 20 mg every 6 h.
pediatrico.notes: Contraindicated in patients with myasthenia gravis, megacolon, and glaucoma. Hyoscine increases the anticholinergic effects of: anticholinergic antidepressants, antihistamines, amantadine, phenothiazines. Concomitant use with metoclopramide may decrease the gastrointestinal effect of both drugs. The ampoule may be administered PO. See alert

stability:
## Pediatric guide

- Discard remainder once opened.
adverseEffects:
## Adverse effects

Dry mouth, tachycardia, visual disturbances, constipation, urinary retention. Allergic reactions.

bibliography.citation: Hospital de Pediatría SAMIC Prof. Dr. Juan P. Garrahan. Institutional Pharmaceutical Formulary — Hyoscine N-Butylbromide (code 0840, ATC A03BA).
